---
# Keep only the privileged elements of an array with arrayfilter + an in() set

**Dataset**: `guardian_xql_lab` (composite/array lookup lab)

`arrayfilter(arr, "@element" in ("admin","root","dba"))` returns a sub-array of only the listed values (the `"@element"` predicate accepts `in (...)`, not just `contains`/`=`). `array_length()` counts the survivors and `arraystring(sub, ",")` flattens them to a readable CSV — together they identify accounts holding privileged roles and how many.

```sql
dataset = guardian_xql_lab
| alter roles = json_extract_scalar_array(roles_json, "$")
| alter priv = arrayfilter(roles, "@element" in ("admin", "root", "dba"))
| alter n_priv = array_length(priv), priv_csv = arraystring(priv, ",")
| filter n_priv > 0
| fields username, priv_csv, n_priv
| sort desc n_priv
```

_Live-verified on a real XSIAM tenant; measured cost ~0.0003 CU against a 10-row lookup (data scanned, not query complexity, drives CU)._
